revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:愛宕神社 (登米市豊里町)]]
{{Infobox religious building | name = Atago Shrine | image = File:Atagojinja.JPG | location = 26 Kotobukizaki, Toyosato Town, {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|en|Tome, Miyagi|qid=Q696401|lt=Tome City|1=Tome, Miyagi}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|qid=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}, Japan | latitude = 38 | lat_minutes = 36 | lat_seconds = 5.75 | longitude = 141 | long_minutes = 14 | long_seconds = 30.39 | deity = [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] (God of Fire) | type = Goshinsha (供進社) | established = {{ill|1651|en|1651|qid=Q6956|lt=1651|1=1651}} (Keian 4) | main_hall = | festival = September 24 | map = Japan Miyagi | prefecture= {{ill|Tome|qid=Q696401|1=Tome, Miyagi}} | coordinates= {{Coord|38.60161111111111|N|141.24177777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Atago Shrine''' (愛宕神社, *Atago Jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Toyosato, Tome, Miyagi|Toyosato Town, Tome City]], {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|qid=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}, Japan<ref>[http://miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?sortmode=&chinzachi=%E7%99%BB%E7%B1%B3%E5%B8%82&jinja_name=&jinja_kana=&jinja_sort_key=t_shrine.kana%2C+t_shrine.address&jinja_sortdesc=&per_page_def=15&page_jinja=0&jinja_cnt=47&code=310030663&PC=1 Atago Shrine - Miyagi Prefecture Shrine Association]</ref>. == Overview == The shrine is believed to provide '''protection against fire''' and '''household safety'''. == History == * '''1651 (Keian 4), June 24''' – '''Genrokudō Naonao''', the eldest son of '''Takeyama Makinosuke Masamitsu''', lord of Aikawa Castle in '''Motoyoshi District''', relocated to '''Akōzu Village, Toyosato''' and established a '''temporary shrine''' at Hakamageyashiki. * '''1680 (Enpō 8), June''' – The '''main shrine building''' was constructed. * '''1795 (Kansei 7), October''' – The '''shrine structures were rebuilt'''. * '''1831 (Tenpō 2)''' – The shrine was '''damaged by fire''', and a '''temporary structure''' was erected. * '''1879 (Meiji 12)''' – The shrine was '''officially designated as a village shrine''' (*sonsha*). * '''1880 (Meiji 13), June 24''' – The '''current shrine hall was rebuilt'''. * '''1903 (Meiji 36)''' – '''Sacred spirits''' were '''transferred from the Yamashiro main shrine''', and '''ritual ceremonies''' were further refined. * '''1944 (Shōwa 19), November 8''' – The shrine was '''designated as a Goshinsha''' (*供進社*). == location == '''Address''': 26 Kotobukizaki, Toyosato Town, '''Tome City, Miyagi Prefecture''', Japan == Nearby Attractions == * '''Hiratsutsumi Marsh''' (平筒沼) * '''Warai-zawa Nature Park''' (笑沢自然公園) * '''Kōrin-ji Temple Gate''' (香林寺山門) * '''Akaōzu Castle Ruins''' (赤生津館跡) == Access == * '''By Car''': ~15 minutes from '''Momonou-Toyosato IC''' on the {{ill|Sanriku Expressway|en|Sanriku Expressway|qid=Q11358269|lt=Sanriku Expressway|1=Sanriku Expressway}}. * '''By Train''': ~30-minute walk from '''Rikuzen-Toyosato Station''' on the {{ill|Kesennuma Line|en|Kesennuma Line|qid=Q872533|lt=Kesennuma Line|1=Kesennuma Line}} ({{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}}). == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310030885 Miyagi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto|qq=}} {{shinto-stub|qq=}} {{Atago Faith|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|愛宕神社 (登米市豊里町)|version=86180227|comment=Imported full ja history}}
